opinion.p: This was an issue joined on the facts alledged in a libel for a divorce; and, upon the trial, the Chief Justice observed, that notice ought to be given of the facts intended to be proved under the general allegations of the libel.
I think it would be most convenient to give notice, that between two specific dates, acts of cruelty &c. were intended to be proved.
The Court seemed to adopt that idea, and recommended it for the future practice of the bar.
